title: At Carnoy
content:
Down in the hollow there's the whole Brigade
    Camped in four groups: through twilight falling slow
    I hear a sound of mouth-organs, ill-played,
    And murmur of voices, gruff, confused, and low.
    Crouched among thistle-tufts I've watched the glow
    Of a blurred orange sunset flare and fade;
    And I'm content. To-morrow we must go
    To take some curs�d Wood.... O world God made!

    July 3rd, 1916.
meaning:
"At Carnoy" is a quintessential example of Siegfried Loraine Sassoon's signature style...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
